notes_md:
**của** means “of” and shows possession, like the ’s in English. It links two nouns to show that something belongs to someone or something.


The thing owned comes first, then **của**, then the owner. You can also use **của + pronoun** alone to mean “mine/yours/his…” after a noun. It’s neutral and works in almost all possession situations.
sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + giữ (keep) + ảnh (photo) + của (of) + gia đình (family)
notes:
của means “of” and shows possession, like the ’s in English. It links two nouns to show that something belongs to someone or something.

The thing owned comes first, then của, then the owner. You can also use của + pronoun alone to mean “mine/yours/his…” after a noun. It’s neutral and works in almost all possession situations.

definitions.en: A word used to indicate possession or belonging.
definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chỉ sự sở hữu hoặc thuộc về ai, cái gì.

exampleSentences: Em giữ ảnh của gia đình.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I keep the photo of the family.
He reads the book of the child.
She carries the bag of her mother.
